rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 21, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF BRYANT FU MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is continued to December 26, 2023 per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. SIMONE ALDERETTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 21, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT SIMONE ALDERETTE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly state what tenancy is being terminated: (1) the "current month to month tenancy based on an oral agreement" (Opposition 3:20") or (2) a tenancy based on a written lease containing the quoted covenant upon which the Notice to Perform Covenant of Quit (Ex 1 to Complaint) is based. The Court does not reach the second basis for the demurrer at this time and it may be raised, if proper, at a later date depending on the allegations in the amended complaint regarding the nature of the current tenancy (based on oral or written agreement) that is being terminated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK P. FARRAGHER VS. MARIA E. LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 21, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT MARIA LOPEZ DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. According to the Notice of the demurrer, the specific basis for the motion is failure "to state a cause of action under the State law." Defendant failed to show that the cause of action under the facts of this case is not stated under the case law. The Court will not address the issue of compliance with SFRO as it is not properly listed in the Notice of Demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RAKESH PATEL VS. EDWIN EDUSMA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 21, 2023 line 6. PLAINTIFF RAKESH PATEL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses to (i) Form Interrogatories - General (Set One), (ii) Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One), (iii) Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), and (iv) Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,160. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
